title: 3 Establishing Policies for Redeeming or Transferring Bonds
body_text:
3	Establishing Policies for Redeeming or Transferring Bonds
3.1	Policy for Redeeming or Transferring Bonds through Central 1

3.2	Policy for Transporting Negotiable Items to Central 1
The use of an armoured car service for shipping negotiable instruments such as Government of Canada Bonds is a requirement for claims made to insurance providers, including CUMIS in Ontario and the Master Bond Program in BC. If the negotiable items are lost, stolen, or damaged in transit using a method of transportation other than armoured car service, neither CUMIS nor the Master Bond Program will cover claims. Credit unions can opt to use alternative means of delivery at their own risk based on the value of the instruments and the credit unionГўВЂВ™s risk policies.
In Ontario, financial institutions are required to purchase fidelity bond coverage, but they can choose from a variety of providers. CUMIS, for example, is one provider available to Ontario credit unions. Financial institutions insured through CUMIS are covered for in-transit items up to $5 million per shipment with an annual aggregate of $10 million. For more information and limitations, contact CUMIS. See Section 1.3, Contacts and Websites.
In BC, the Master Bond Program is mandatory. In-transit items via armoured vehicle are insured up to $60 million per claim with an annual aggregate of $120 million. For more information and limitations, contact Stabilization Central Credit Union (Master Bond Program). See Section 1.3, Contacts and Websites.
Central 1 will not assume responsibility for lost, damaged, or stolen securities that are shipped by an alternate method. Central 1 also doesnГўВЂВ™t accept registered mail. Central 1 recommends using a courier shipping service as an alternate method if armoured car service canГўВЂВ™t be used. At a minimum, it is recommended that the selected shipping method require a signature by Central 1 upon receipt of the items.
The financial institution is responsible for the transportation costs since a member cannot be charged fees for redeeming a bond. Before shipping, securities must be scanned or photocopied to identify each item.
3.3	Policy for Retaining Source Documents
It is the financial institutionГўВЂВ™s responsibility to set up policies regarding the retention of source documents processed via Branch Capture. CPA Rule 10 indicates that source documents such as bond certificates are to be destroyed within 120 calendar days of image creation.
END
